title: ephemerantha lonchophylla
intro: Ephemerantha lonchophylla, also known as the "fragrant orchid," is a type of flowering plant in the orchid family that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Ephemerantha lonchophylla are primarily attributed to its essential oils, which have been used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of this plant include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Ephemerantha lonchophylla often involve infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water to create teas or decoctions that can be used to treat a range of health complaints.
